quote: You have power over your mind - not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The best revenge is to be unlike him who performed the injury.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The soul becomes dyed with the color of its thoughts.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: It is not death that a man should fear, but he should fear never beginning to live.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Reject your sense of injury and the injury itself disappears.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: When you arise in the morning think of what a privilege it is to be alive, to think, to enjoy, to love.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Very little is needed to make a happy life; it is all within yourself in your way of thinking.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

quote: As long as you live, keep learning how to live.
author: Seneca

quote: True happiness is to enjoy the present, without anxious dependence upon the future.
author: Seneca

quote: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
author: Seneca

quote: All cruelty springs from weakness.
author: Seneca

quote: Difficulties strengthen the mind, as labor does the body.
author: Seneca

quote: If a man knows not to which port he sails, no wind is favorable.
author: Seneca

quote: It is the power of the mind to be unconquerable.
author: Seneca

quote: No man is more unhappy than he who never faces adversity, for he is not permitted to prove himself.
author: Seneca

quote: He suffers more than necessary who suffers before it is necessary.
author: Seneca

quote: We cannot choose our external circumstances, but we can always choose how we respond to them.
author: Epictetus

quote: It's not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
author: Epictetus

quote: No man is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

quote: Man is not worried by real problems so much as by his imagined anxieties about real problems.
author: Epictetus

quote: First learn the meaning of what you say, and then speak.
author: Epictetus

quote: If you want to improve, be content to be thought foolish and stupid.
author: Epictetus

quote: Any person capable of angering you becomes your master.
author: Epictetus

quote: He who laughs at himself never runs out of things to laugh at.
author: Epictetus

quote: Freedom is the only worthy goal in life. It is won by disregarding things that lie beyond our control.
author: Epictetus

quote: Only the educated are free.
author: Epictetus

quote: Look within. Within is the fountain of good, and it will ever bubble up, if thou wilt ever dig.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Keep your attention focused entirely on what is truly your own concern.
author: Epictetus

quote: A gift consists not in what is done or given, but in the intention of the giver or doer.
author: Seneca

quote: The primary sign of a well-ordered mind is a man's ability to remain in one place and linger in his own company.
author: Seneca

quote: Nowhere can man find a quieter or more untroubled retreat than in his own soul.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Accept the things to which fate binds you, and love the people with whom fate brings you together, but do so with all your heart.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: He who is brave is free.
author: Seneca

quote: If it is not right do not do it; if it is not true do not say it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Choose not to be harmed—and you won't feel harmed. Don't feel harmed—and you haven't been.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The best wealth is a poverty of desires.
author: Seneca

quote: Don't explain your philosophy. Embody it.
author: Epictetus

quote: The minor things do not matter, only your virtue matters.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Wellbeing is attained by little and little, and yet is no little thing itself.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Man conquers the world by conquering himself.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Steep is the ascent, but the view is magnificent.
author: Seneca

quote: We have two ears and one mouth so that we can listen twice as much as we speak.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: No loss should be more severely felt than that of time, for it is irretrievable.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Steel your mind against external disasters.
author: Chrysippus

quote: The wise man is self-sufficient.
author: Chrysippus

quote: If you choose, you are free; if you choose, you need blame no one.
author: Epictetus

quote: Associate with people who are likely to improve you.
author: Seneca

quote: Let all your things have their places; let each part of your business have its time.
author: Epictetus

quote: To be calm is the highest achievement of the self.
author: Zenodotus

quote: Virtue is the only true good.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To live a good life: We have the potential for it. If we can learn to be indifferent to what makes no difference.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Think of yourself as dead. You have lived your life. Now, take what's left and live it properly.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Receive without conceit, release without struggle.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Loss is nothing else but change, and change is Natures delight.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The object of life is not to be on the side of the majority, but to escape finding oneself in the ranks of the insane.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Because your own strength is unequal to the task, do not assume that it is beyond the powers of man.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The precision of our duties matches the clarity of our reason.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Nothing happens to anybody which he is not fitted by nature to bear.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: When force of circumstance upsets your equanimity, lose no time in recovering your self-control.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not build external walls when you require internal anchors.
author: Seneca

quote: Begin each day by telling yourself: Today I shall be meeting with interference, ingratitude, insolence, disloyalty, ill-will, and selfishness.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: That which is not good for the bee-hive cannot be good for the bee.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: He who lives in harmony with himself lives in harmony with the universe.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The memory of everything is very soon overwhelmed in time.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Every soul is deprived of truth against its will.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Time is a sort of river of passing events, and strong is its current.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To understand the true quality of people, look into their minds, examine their pursuits and aversions.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Whatever happens to you has been waiting to happen since the beginning of time.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do every act of your life as though it were the very last act of your life.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The universal nature has given to each rational being the power of conforming its actions to the reason.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Be like the cliff against which the waves continually break; but it stands firm and tames the fury of the water around it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: In a word, your life is short. Make the most of the present with aid of reason and justice.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The mind which is free from passions is a fortress, for man has nothing more secure to which he can fly.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A man's worth is no greater than his ambitions.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Look back over the past, with its changing empires that rose and fell, and you can foresee the future, too.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Adapt yourself to the life you have been given and truly love the people with whom fate has surrounded you.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not indulge in dreams of having what you have not, but reckon up the chief of the blessings you do possess.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Never let the future disturb you. You will meet it, if you have to, with the same weapons of reason which today arm you against the present.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: People are local, but virtue is universal.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Accustom yourself to attend carefully to what is said by another, and as much as it is possible, be within the speaker's mind.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: What an endless space of time has preceded us, and what an infinity will follow.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Everything that happens happens justly. If you observe carefully, you will find this to be true.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not be ashamed of admitting help. It is your task to achieve your duty, like a soldier on the rampart.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The best way to avenge yourself is to not be like that.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Examine the men who are called successful, and you will find they are driven by temporary desires.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: No form of vessel is shattered if its interior remains strong against pressure.
author: Seneca

quote: The condition of a citizen requires that he value his own sanity above the praise of crowds.
author: Epictetus

quote: Is your cucumber bitter? Throw it away. Are there briars in your path? Turn aside. That is enough.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If any man is able to convince me and show me that I do not think or act right, I will gladly change.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Such as are your habitual thoughts, such also will be the character of your mind.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To love only what happens, what was destined. No greater harmony exists.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not act as if you were going to live ten thousand years. Death hangs over you.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: How much time he gains who does not look to see what his neighbour says or does or thinks.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Everything which is in any way beautiful is beautiful in itself, and terminates in itself, not having praise as part of itself.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not scramble through life; let your actions be complete, your speech plain, and your thoughts deep.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: He who uncovers truth uncovers a refuge from anxiety.
author: Seneca

quote: No man is crushed by misfortune unless he has first been deceived by prosperity.
author: Seneca

quote: We should every night call ourselves to an account: What infirmity have I mastered today?
author: Seneca

quote: Expect to encounter small minds; resolve to retain a great one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Enjoy your own life without comparing it with that of another.
author: Seneca

quote: Most powerful is he who has himself in his own power.
author: Seneca

quote: The path of virtue is narrow, but it leads to heights unmatched by greed.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: A large part of goodness is the desire to become good.
author: Seneca

quote: You must expect everything to happen, and steel your mind for all outcomes.
author: Seneca

quote: Fire tests gold, adversity tests brave men.
author: Seneca

quote: The mind that is anxious about future events is miserable.
author: Seneca

quote: Sorrow is a debt we pay to our own lack of perspective.
author: Epictetus

quote: Nothing is honorable which is not also just.
author: Seneca

quote: It is not the man who has too little, but the man who craves more, that is poor.
author: Seneca

quote: Our duty is to act well our given part; the selection of the part belongs to another.
author: Epictetus

quote: The greatest remedy for anger is delay.
author: Seneca

quote: To be always fortunate, and to pass through life with a soul that has never known sorrow, is to be ignorant of one half of nature.
author: Seneca

quote: While we are postponing, life speeds by.
author: Seneca

quote: There is no enjoying the possession of anything valuable unless one has a mind companionable with its loss.
author: Seneca

quote: Life is very short and anxious for those who forget the past, neglect the present, and fear the future.
author: Seneca

quote: He who is a friend to himself is also a friend to all men.
author: Seneca

quote: Until we have begun to go without them, we fail to realize how unnecessary many things are.
author: Seneca

quote: It is a rough road that leads to the heights of greatness.
author: Seneca

quote: If you wish to be loved, love.
author: Seneca

quote: A room without books is like a body without a soul, but a mind without restraint is a body without a pilot.
author: Seneca

quote: The fidelity of a friend is more valuable than all the wealth of an empire.
author: Seneca

quote: The part of life we really live is small. For all the rest of existence is not life, but merely time.
author: Seneca

quote: Every night, let your actions submit to internal trial.
author: Seneca

quote: Regard a friend as loyal, and you will make him loyal.
author: Seneca

quote: He who runs from fate runs from his own highest good.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Quiet minds cannot be perplexed or frightened by external thunder.
author: Seneca

quote: Quiet minds cannot be perplexed or frightened by external thunder.
author: Seneca

quote: Nothing is so wretched or foolish as to anticipate misfortune.
author: Seneca

quote: Men do not care how nobly they live, but only how long, although it is within the reach of every man to live nobly.
author: Seneca

quote: To bear trials with a calm mind robs misfortune of its strength and burden.
author: Seneca

quote: If you are detached from outcomes, you are safe from disappointments.
author: Epictetus

quote: He who has made the world his community has no reason to fear isolation.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: No evil is honorable; but death is honorable; therefore death is not an evil.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: The goal of life is living in agreement with nature.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: A bad feeling is a commotion of the mind repugnant to reason, and against nature.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: The avaricious man is like the barren sandy ground of the desert which sucks in all the rain and dews with greediness.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Follow where your reason guides, and let the opinions of fools fall away like dust.
author: Chrysippus

quote: When a man speaks without understanding, his words are merely noise.
author: Chrysippus

quote: There are things which are within our power, and there are things which are beyond our power.
author: Epictetus

quote: Control your passions lest they take vengeance on you.
author: Epictetus

quote: No person is free who is not master of himself.
author: Epictetus

quote: If evil be spoken of thee, and it be true, correct thyself; if it be a lie, laugh at it.
author: Epictetus

quote: Wish not that things should happen as you wish, but wish the things which happen to be as they are.
author: Epictetus

quote: Neither should a ship rely on one small anchor, nor should life depend on a single hope.
author: Epictetus

quote: Small minds blame others. Average minds blame themselves. Wise minds see no one to blame.
author: Epictetus

quote: God has entrusted me with myself. No one can steal that trust.
author: Epictetus

quote: If you want something good, get it from yourself.
author: Epictetus

quote: It is the act of an ill-instructed man to blame others for his own bad condition.
author: Epictetus

quote: Only the man who desires nothing external can be truly independent.
author: Epictetus

quote: Attach yourself to what is spiritually superior, regardless of what other people think or do.
author: Epictetus

quote: Seek not the good in outward things; seek it in yourselves.
author: Epictetus

quote: Men are disturbed not by things, but by the views which they take of things.
author: Epictetus

quote: To accuse others for one's own misfortunes is a sign of want of education.
author: Epictetus

quote: When you are offended at any man's fault, turn to yourself and study your own failings.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: True freedom is the ability to choose your own attitude in any circumstance.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not look to the theatres or the plazas for validation; find it in your own judgment.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: If you speak truth, you need not remember your past declarations.
author: Epictetus

quote: Choose rather to punish your appetites than to be punished by them.
author: Epictetus

quote: Practice yourself, for heaven's sake, in little things; and thence proceed to greater.
author: Epictetus

quote: It is impossible for a man to learn what he thinks he already knows.
author: Epictetus

quote: When we invite an external problem to ruin our day, we betray our internal governance.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Give me a single man who cares how he does what he does, and is interested rather in the execution than the outcome.
author: Epictetus

quote: Know you not that a good man does nothing for appearance' sake, but for the sake of having done right?
author: Epictetus

quote: True philosophy lies in actions, not in grand orations.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The primary task in life is simply this: to identify and separate matters so that I can say clearly which are externals.
author: Epictetus

quote: He who fawns over rulers trades his mind for safety.
author: Epictetus

quote: We must not mind the insults of men who lack knowledge of virtue.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The source of all human misery is the misapplication of general principles to particular cases.
author: Epictetus

quote: If anyone tells you that a certain person speaks ill of you, do not make excuses but answer: He was ignorant of my other faults.
author: Epictetus

quote: A citizen of the world knows no limits to his compassion or his responsibility.
author: Epictetus

quote: No labor is too great if it results in the correction of your character.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: When you have shut your doors, and darkened your room, remember never to say that you are alone, for you are not; God is within.
author: Epictetus

quote: To live a life of pleasure is to live a life of dependence.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The shield of the wise man is his indifference to the arrows of praise.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The mind is not a vessel to be filled, but a hearth to be guarded.
author: Epictetus

quote: If you would have your mind at peace, be ready to be considered simple and foolish.
author: Epictetus

quote: Difficulties are things that show what men are.
author: Epictetus

quote: Let death and exile and everything which appears terrible be daily before your eyes.
author: Epictetus

quote: Never say of anything, 'I have lost it'; but, 'I have returned it.'
author: Epictetus

quote: Be satisfied with being a philosopher; and if you wish to seem so to anyone, seem so to yourself.
author: Epictetus

quote: The true test of a man is how he bears himself when the world turns against him.
author: Seneca

quote: Let not your mind wander from the task at hand; focus is the root of excellence.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: In every circumstance, keep your primary directives clean.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A healthy mind does not require gold or praise to demonstrate its perfection.
author: Seneca

quote: An unexamined life is a journey through thick fog.
author: Epictetus

quote: Be content with what you are, and wish not change; nor dread your last day, nor long for it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To complain about fate is to show yourself a raw recruit in the army of nature.
author: Seneca

quote: The universe is change; our life is what our thoughts make it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The soul is like a city under siege: if the walls are weak, the enemy enters.
author: Chrysippus

quote: Let your desires be governed by reason, not by the shifting whims of the crowd.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not seek to outshine others in luxury; seek to outshine them in wisdom.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The mind that is free from desire is an impregnable citadel.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Nothing is so burdensome as a mind that cannot govern its own appetites.
author: Seneca

quote: A truly noble soul does not fear death, but rather a life devoid of purpose.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Look at the stars and see yourself running with them.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: If you wish to be free from anxiety, live each day as if it were your entire life.
author: Seneca

quote: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not let the impressions of the world sweep you off your feet.
author: Epictetus

quote: Every difficulty is an opportunity to practice a new virtue.
author: Epictetus

quote: The soul's true beauty lies in its adherence to justice and truth.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A wise man adapts to his circumstances as water shapes itself to the vessel.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Do not be distracted by the clamor of the marketplace; your sanctuary is within.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: True strength lies not in the absence of emotion, but in the mastery of it.
author: Seneca

quote: The mind that is steady can look upon any disaster without flinching.
author: Chrysippus

quote: A life spent chasing shadows is a life wasted.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not complain about the darkness; be the one who brings the light.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The wise man is never lonely, for he is always in the company of his own thoughts.
author: Seneca

quote: Let your actions match your words, and your words match your thoughts.
author: Epictetus

quote: Nothing external can harm you unless you allow it entry into your soul.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The path to peace is found by surrendering what you cannot control.
author: Epictetus

quote: A man who fears nothing has already conquered the world.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not build your house on the shifting sands of public opinion.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The soul is dyed with the color of its deeds.
author: Chrysippus

quote: To live in accordance with nature is to live in perfect harmony.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: A wise man seeks nothing beyond his own moral character.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not let yesterday's failures cast a shadow over today's resolve.
author: Seneca

quote: The mind is its own place, and can make a heaven of hell or a hell of heaven.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: True wealth is not measured by possessions, but by the absence of greed.
author: Seneca

quote: The only real harm that can come to you is the corruption of your own character.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not wait for the perfect moment; take the moment and make it perfect.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A steadfast mind is a shield against all the arrows of fortune.
author: Seneca

quote: The universe is a single living being; act always as a part of it.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not let the behavior of others disturb your inner peace.
author: Epictetus

quote: A life of virtue is its own reward.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: The mind that is anchored in reason cannot be moved by passion.
author: Chrysippus

quote: Do not waste your breath on those who refuse to listen to reason.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The wise man looks inward for validation, never outward.
author: Seneca

quote: To master your thoughts is to master your destiny.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The only things that belong to you are your actions and your judgments.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not let the fear of death rob you of the joy of living.
author: Seneca

quote: A clear conscience is the softest pillow.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The soul that is pure sees the world as it truly is.
author: Chrysippus

quote: To live simply is to live wisely.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The mind that is steady can weather any storm.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not seek to change the world; seek to change yourself.
author: Epictetus

quote: A man's true worth is shown in his treatment of his enemies.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The universe is governed by laws; find peace in accepting them.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Do not let the illusions of grandeur lead you astray.
author: Seneca

quote: The only true freedom is freedom from fear.
author: Epictetus

quote: A mind focused on virtue is a mind at rest.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not let the judgments of the ignorant affect your resolve.
author: Chrysippus

quote: A life of integrity requires no justification.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The wise man treats every obstacle as a stepping stone.
author: Seneca

quote: To know yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
author: Epictetus

quote: The soul is an eternal flame; do not let it be extinguished by neglect.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not look back with regret; look forward with determination.
author: Seneca

quote: The mind that is disciplined is a powerful ally.
author: Chrysippus

quote: To find happiness, limit your desires to what is within your reach.
author: Epictetus

quote: A man of character stands firm even when the ground gives way.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not chase after fleeting pleasures; chase after lasting peace.
author: Seneca

quote: The world is a mirror; it reflects back your own inner state.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: To be content with the present is the ultimate wisdom.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not let the malice of others corrupt your kindness.
author: Epictetus

quote: A noble character is the best legacy you can leave behind.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The mind that is free is the only mind that can think clearly.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not fear hardship; it is the forge in which character is shaped.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The only limits that exist are the ones you place on yourself.
author: Epictetus

quote: A steady hand and a calm mind can accomplish anything.
author: Chrysippus

quote: To live with honor is to live without fear.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not let the weight of the world crush your spirit.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The wise man accepts what cannot be changed and moves forward.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: To find truth, look beneath the surface of things.
author: Epictetus

quote: A disciplined mind is a refuge from all worldly chaos.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not seek applause; seek the approval of your own conscience.
author: Seneca

quote: The soul's journey is inward, not outward.
author: Chrysippus

quote: To be at peace with yourself is to be at peace with the world.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The only true mastery is the mastery of one's own desires.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not let the distractions of life derail your purpose.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A mind anchored in truth cannot be shaken by falsehood.
author: Seneca

quote: To live a life of virtue is to live a life of true freedom.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: Do not look for happiness in the hands of others; it is in your own.
author: Epictetus

quote: A strong character is built one choice at a time.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The universe operates in perfect order; find your place within it.
author: Chrysippus

quote: Do not let the opinion of the crowd dictate your worth.
author: Seneca

quote: A clear vision and a steady heart will guide you through any darkness.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To master the self is the greatest of all victories.
author: Epictetus

quote: Do not let the small things steal your joy.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: A wise man guards his mind as a treasure.
author: Seneca

quote: The path of virtue is its own light.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: To live without pretense is to live with true power.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: Do not be enslaved by your passions; be liberated by your reason.
author: Epictetus

quote: A soul that is at peace radiates strength.
author: Chrysippus

quote: To stand alone for what is right is the ultimate courage.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not let the passage of time diminish your resolve.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: The only true wealth is the wealth of the soul.
author: Epictetus

quote: A quiet mind can find beauty in the simplest things.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: To live each day with intention is the mark of a wise man.
author: Seneca

quote: Do not let the noise of the world drown out your inner voice.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: A mind that is flexible can never be broken.
author: Zeno of Citium

quote: The path to tranquility is paved with acceptance.
author: Epictetus

quote: A noble heart finds joy in the welfare of others.
author: Chrysippus

quote: Do not seek to escape reality; seek to master it.
author: Seneca

quote: The soul is the pilot; let reason be the map.
author: Marcus Aurelius

quote: To find true freedom, detach yourself from worldly outcomes.
author: Epictetus

quote: A life grounded in virtue stands unshaken by any storm.
author: Musonius Rufus

quote: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single, mindful step.
author: Seneca
